edges.description: Circulating autoantibodies bind podocyte antigens and seed local immune-complex formation along the outer glomerular basement membrane.
edges.target: In situ subepithelial immune complex deposition

edges.description: Subepithelial immune deposits trigger complement activation around podocytes and the outer glomerular basement membrane.
edges.source: In situ subepithelial immune complex deposition
edges.target: Complement activation at the glomerular capillary wall

edges.description: Complement effector activity injures podocytes and destabilizes the slit-diaphragm/cytoskeletal apparatus.
edges.source: Complement activation at the glomerular capillary wall

edges.description: Podocyte injury reduces filtration selectivity and allows sustained urinary protein loss.
edges.target: Glomerular filtration barrier failure and proteinuria

edges.description: Ongoing nephrotic-range proteinuria and persistent glomerular injury are associated with reduced kidney survival.
edges.source: Glomerular filtration barrier failure and proteinuria

nodes.description: Primary membranous nephropathy reflects loss of tolerance to podocyte autoantigens, with B cell and plasma cell responses generating circulating autoantibodies against PLA2R1 in most seropositive cases and THSD7A in a smaller minority. These serologic subsets are mechanistically important for primary disease classification and monitoring, but they do not imply that the entire membranous nephropathy disease root is monogenic. Other target antigens are tracked at the subtype level so the root pathograph stays focused on the shared autoimmune cascade rather than fragmenting into parallel sparse upstream nodes.

evidence.snippet: Approximately 50-80% and 3-5% of primary MN (PMN) cases are associated with either anti-PLA2R or anti-THSD7A antibodies, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: Immunological Pathogenesis of Membranous Nephropathy: Focus on PLA2R1 and Its Role.
evidence.snippet: In idiopathic membranous nephropathy (IMN) the immune complexes are formed by circulating antibodies binding mainly to one of two naturally-expressed podocyte antigens: the M-type receptor for secretory phospholipase A2 (PLA2R1) and the Thrombospondin type-1 domain-containing 7A (THSD7A).

nodes.description: The subepithelial immune deposits activate complement at the podocyte-facing glomerular basement membrane, generating membrane attack complex and C5a signals that amplify local injury.
nodes.id: Complement activation at the glomerular capillary wall

evidence.reference_title: [Modern view on the complement system role in membranous nephropathy].
evidence.snippet: Membrane-attacking complex (MAC) is the terminal product of any complement pathways activation (classical, lectin or alternative) and plays the leading role in the complement-mediated podocytic damage.

evidence.reference_title: Autophagy in the Pathogenesis of Membranous Nephropathy.
evidence.snippet: The formation of immune complexes on podocytes triggers complement activation, leading to the activation of both the C5b-C9 complex and C5a.

nodes.description: Cyclophosphamide-based regimens remain standard high-risk therapy for primary membranous nephropathy.

evidence.reference_title: Ten tips on immunosuppression in primary membranous nephropathy.
evidence.snippet: Third, we suggest prescribing rituximab (RTX) or RTX combined with calcineurin inhibitors in medium-risk patients. Fourth, we propose cyclophosphamide-based immunosuppression for high-risk patients.

nodes.description: Once podocytes are injured, the glomerular filtration barrier loses permselectivity and patients develop the heavy proteinuria that drives the nephrotic syndrome phenotype. Persistent proteinuria is also linked to worse long-term kidney outcomes.
nodes.id: Glomerular filtration barrier failure and proteinuria

evidence.reference_title: [Modern view on the complement system role in membranous nephropathy].
evidence.snippet: In MN, proteinuria is developed by podocyte damage due to the complement system activation in response to the subepithelial deposition of immune complexes containing various auto- and exogenous antigens.

nodes.description: Autoantibodies bind antigens exposed on the podocyte-facing aspect of the glomerular capillary wall, generating immune complexes in situ. Those complexes accumulate as subepithelial electron-dense deposits and are accompanied by progressive thickening of the glomerular basement membrane.
nodes.id: In situ subepithelial immune complex deposition

evidence.reference_title: The fate of immune complexes in membranous nephropathy.
evidence.snippet: The most characteristic feature of membranous nephropathy (MN) is the presence of subepithelial electron dense deposits and the consequential thickening of the glomerular basement membrane.

evidence.reference_title: The fate of immune complexes in membranous nephropathy.
evidence.snippet: Subepithelial immune complexes are formed in situ by autoantibodies targeting native autoantigens or exogenous planted antigens such as the phospholipase A2 receptor (PLA2R) and cationic BSA respectively.
Membranous nephropathy (MN) is an autoimmune disease of the kidney glomerulus and one of the leading causes of nephrotic syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: HLA-DQA1 and PLA2R1 polymorphisms and risk of idiopathic membranous nephropathy.
evidence.snippet: Single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) within HLA complex class II HLA-DQ α-chain 1 (HLA-DQA1) and M-type phospholipase A2 receptor (PLA2R1) genes were identified as strong risk factors for idiopathic membranous nephropathy (IMN) development in a recent genome-wide association study.

nodes.description: Complement-dependent and direct antibody-mediated injury disrupt podocyte structural homeostasis, including actin cytoskeletal organization and slit-diaphragm integrity. This converts a subepithelial deposit pattern into a filtration-barrier lesion.

evidence.reference_title: Immune-mediated membranous nephropathy: Innovations in pathogenetic modeling and mechanistic insights.
evidence.snippet: Collectively, they reveal complement-dependent and direct podocytotoxic injury mechanisms.

evidence.reference_title: [Modern view on the complement system role in membranous nephropathy].
evidence.snippet: In MN, proteinuria is developed by podocyte damage due to the complement system activation in response to the subepithelial deposition of immune complexes containing various auto- and exogenous antigens.

nodes.description: Clinical course is heterogeneous, but a subset of patients experiences sustained eGFR decline and kidney failure despite modern therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Risk Factors for Disease Progression for Adults and Children With Membranous Nephropathy in the Cure Glomerulonephropathy Network (CureGN).
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: In total, 591 patients (537 adults and 54 children) were evaluated with 9% reaching kidney failure.

evidence.reference_title: [Modern view on the complement system role in membranous nephropathy].
evidence.snippet: In MN, proteinuria is developed by podocyte damage due to the complement system activation in response to the subepithelial deposition of immune complexes containing various auto- and exogenous antigens.

evidence.reference_title: Risk Factors for Disease Progression for Adults and Children With Membranous Nephropathy in the Cure Glomerulonephropathy Network (CureGN).
evidence.snippet: Age < 18 years, self-reported Black/African American race, proteinuria > 3 g/g, and lower estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) at enrollment were associated with worse kidney survival.

nodes.description: Anti-CD20 B-cell depletion used in guideline-supported first-line immunotherapy for primary membranous nephropathy.

evidence.reference_title: Rituximab or Cyclosporine in the Treatment of Membranous Nephropathy.
evidence.snippet: Rituximab was noninferior to cyclosporine in inducing complete or partial remission of proteinuria at 12 months and was superior in maintaining proteinuria remission up to 24 months.

evidence.reference_title: The Use of Rituximab in Glomerulonephritis: What Is the Evidence?
evidence.snippet: Recent evidence and the KDIGO guideline establish Rituximab as the first-line treatment for Primary Membranous Nephropathy and ANCA-associated vasculitis.

nodes.description: Supportive therapy aimed at reducing non-immune kidney injury progression, including renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system blockade and SGLT2 inhibition.

evidence.reference_title: CKD therapy to improve outcomes of immune-mediated glomerular diseases.
evidence.snippet: Approved drug interventions include inhibitors of the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system and sodium-glucose cotransporter-2.
